option.name: fileSummaryMaps
option.value: {"C:/ANKIT/Practice":"{\"/src/practice/Occurences.java\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following function:\\n\\n`main`: This function takes a string \\\"mediterranean\\\" and counts the occurrences of each character in the string, storing the results in a HashMap, and then prints out the key-value pairs of the HashMap.\\n\\nThe file is a simple Java program that demonstrates how to use a HashMap to count the occurrences of characters in a string.\",\"/src/practice/MapVsFlatMap.java\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`main`: This function demonstrates the difference between the `map` and `flatMap` operations in Java streams.\\n\\nThe file demonstrates the use of `map` and `flatMap` operations on Java streams. The `map` operation is used to transform each element of a stream, while the `flatMap` operation is used to flatten a stream of streams into a single stream. The file shows how these operations can be used to perform different types of transformations on collections of data.\",\"/src/practice/SingletonExample.java\":\"This file implements the Singleton design pattern, which ensures that a class has only one instance and provides a global point of access to it.\\n\\n`SingletonExample`: A class that ensures only one instance of itself is created and provides a global point of access to it.\\n\\n`main`: The main method that demonstrates the Singleton pattern by creating two instances of the `SingletonExample` class and printing their hash codes.\\n\\n`getInstance`: A static method that returns the single instance of the `SingletonExample` class, creating it if it doesn\\u0027t already exist.\\n\\n`SingletonExample`: A private constructor that prevents the direct creation of `SingletonExample` objects, ensuring that the class can only be instantiated through the `getInstance` method.\",\"/src/practice/InterfaceExample.java\":\"`InterfaceExample`: An interface that defines a `process()` method and a default `show()` method.\\n\\n`TestDefault`: A class that implements the `InterfaceExample` interface and provides an implementation for the `process()` method.\\n\\n`main()`: The entry point of the program, which creates an instance of `TestDefault`, calls the `process()` method, and then calls the `show()` method.\",\"/src/practice/streams/ArraysPractice.java\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`main`: This function demonstrates the usage of the `Arrays.stream()` method to perform various operations on an array of integers, such as limiting the stream, sorting, and skipping elements.\\n\\nThe file demonstrates the usage of the Java Streams API to perform common operations on an array of integers, such as printing the first 5 elements, sorting the array and printing the second element, and skipping the first element and printing the second element.\",\"/src/practice/Anagram.java\":\"This file contains a single class `Anagram` and a single function `checkAnagram()`.\\n\\n`Anagram`: This class contains a main method that checks if two given strings are anagrams of each other.\\n\\n`checkAnagram()`: This function takes two strings as input and returns `true` if they are anagrams, and `false` otherwise.\",\"/src/practice/BubbleSort.java\":\"This file contains a single class and a single function:\\n\\n`BubbleSort`: This class implements the bubble sort algorithm to sort an array of integers.\\n\\n`sortNumbers`: This function takes an array of integers as input, sorts the array using the bubble sort algorithm, and returns the sorted array.\\n\\nThe file as a whole demonstrates the implementation of the bubble sort algorithm in Java, which is a simple sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements, and swaps them if they are in the wrong order.\",\"/src/practice/streams/IntermediatePractice.java\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following functions:\\n\\n`main`: This function demonstrates the usage of various intermediate operations in Java Streams, such as sorting, reversing, and reducing a list of integers.\\n\\nThe file demonstrates the following:\\n1. Sorting a list of integers in ascending order using the `sorted()` method.\\n2. Sorting a list of integers in descending order using the `sorted()` method with `Collections.reverseOrder()`.\\n3. Reducing a list of integers to a single sum value using the `reduce()` method.\",\"/src/practice/Reverse.java\":\"This file contains a single class `Reverse` and two functions `reverseString` and `reverseNumber`.\\n\\n`Reverse`: This class demonstrates how to reverse a string and a number.\\n\\n`reverseString`: This function takes a string as input and prints the reversed string.\\n\\n`reverseNumber`: This function takes an integer as input and prints the reversed number.\",\"/src/practice/streams/FilterPractice.java\":\"This file contains a Java program that demonstrates the use of Java Streams to filter a list of `Person` objects based on their age.\\n\\n`Person`: A class that represents a person with a name and age.\\n\\n`main()`: The main function that creates a list of `Person` objects, filters the list to get the people above 50 years of age, and prints the resulting list.\",\"/src/practice/Palindrome.java\":\"This file contains a single class `Palindrome` and two functions `palindromeString` and `palindromeNumber`.\\n\\n`Palindrome`: This class contains methods to check if a given number or string is a palindrome.\\n\\n`palindromeString`: This function takes a string as input and checks if it is a palindrome by comparing characters from the beginning and end of the string.\\n\\n`palindromeNumber`: This function takes an integer as input and checks if it is a palindrome by reversing the number and comparing it with the original number.\",\"/src/practice/Test.java\":\"This file does not contain any classes. It contains the following function:\\n\\n`main(String[] args)`: This function creates a list of strings, filters the list to include only the strings that start with \\\"A\\\", and then prints the filtered list.\\n\\nThe file demonstrates the use of Java streams to filter a list of strings and collect the filtered results into a new list.\"}"}
